title: Average GDD requiring implementable technical design

inputs.summary: A top-down survival game GDD defines movement, enemies, pickups, and score, but lacks system boundaries, data model, Unity scene mapping, and acceptance criteria.

documents.content: Player moves with WASD, avoids enemies, collects batteries, and survives for three minutes. Batteries increase score and enemies end the run on contact.

expectedObservations: Systems should be split into player movement, enemy contact, pickup scoring, timer, run state, and HUD.
Data model should distinguish authored tuning from runtime state.
Acceptance criteria should cover movement, pickup scoring, enemy failure, and timer win.
expectedRecommendations: Create Technical Design with component contracts.
failureModes: Produces broad documentation without component or data boundaries.
Adds unsupported survival systems such as crafting or base building.
Fails to map requirements to Unity scenes, prefabs, scripts, or data.
